name: One Level of Abstraction per Function
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: Mixing high-level concepts (getHtml()) with low-level details (.append("\n")) inside the same function makes readers unable to tell what is essential logic vs. an implementation detail, and details accumulate over time.
steps: Identify the abstraction level of the function from its name.
Ensure every statement inside operates exactly one level below that abstraction.
Extract any statement that is at a lower abstraction level into a helper function.
Apply the Stepdown Rule: arrange functions so each is followed by the next lower level of abstraction.
Read the function as a TO paragraph; if details intrude, extract them.
